sources: Department for Communities NI — Historic Environment Fund launch

funds.name: National Lottery Heritage Fund — Heritage Places
funds.niTrackRecord: Over £290 million awarded since 1994 to almost 2,000 projects in Northern Ireland

programmes.name: National Lottery Project Grants
programmes.fernhillUse: Phase A urgent works or early community engagement programme

programmes.name: Heritage Places
programmes.window: Two-stage: EOI → Stage 1 Development → Stage 2 Delivery
programmes.fernhillUse: Primary Phase B capital grant — anchor funder

funds.contact: National Lottery Heritage Fund, 4th Floor, 1 Lanyon Place, Belfast BT1 3LP

funds.name: Architectural Heritage Fund
funds.description: NI-specific programme supporting community-led heritage building regeneration.

programmes.name: Project Development Grant
programmes.fernhillUse: Phase 1 feasibility: professional building survey, business plan, architectural design

programmes.name: Viability and Capacity Grant
programmes.fernhillUse: Governance setup and early capacity building during founding trustee period

funds.fernhillTarget: £50,000–£80,000 Project Development Grant — FIRST APPLICATION TO SUBMIT

funds.name: Historic Environment Fund — NI
funds.launchNote: Launched by Communities Minister Gordon Lyons — new NI Executive programme
funds.applicationRoute: Via Historic Environment Division (HED), Department for Communities
funds.fernhillTarget: £100,000–£400,000 for Phase A and B structural works

funds.name: SEUPB PEACE PLUS Programme
funds.body: Special EU Programmes Body (SEUPB), delivered via Belfast City Council
funds.belfastAllocation: £15.1 million (€17.4 million) awarded to Belfast PEACEPLUS Local Community Action Plan
funds.fernhillFit: VERY HIGH — Fernhill's cross-community model (serving all Belfast communities for weddings, wakes, community events) directly meets PEACEPLUS criteria
funds.fernhillTarget: £100,000–£300,000 for community spaces, remembrance garden, cross-community programming

funds.name: Belfast City Council — Community and Regeneration Grants

programmes.name: Belfast Region City Deal — Heritage component
programmes.notes: £1.2bn City Deal with cultural and heritage investment components

philanthropicFunders.name: Community Foundation for Northern Ireland
philanthropicFunders.notes: Manages multiple donor funds including Dormant Accounts Fund NI. Heritage and community alignment.

philanthropicFunders.name: The Pilgrim Trust
philanthropicFunders.notes: Heritage buildings core mandate. Has funded NI heritage projects.

philanthropicFunders.name: Wolfson Foundation
philanthropicFunders.notes: Strong heritage capital grant track record across UK.

philanthropicFunders.name: Esmée Fairbairn Foundation
philanthropicFunders.notes: Major UK heritage and arts funder.

philanthropicFunders.name: The American Ireland Fund
philanthropicFunders.notes: Peace, heritage, culture in Ireland and NI. Belfast diaspora in USA. Tax-efficient for US donors via AIF.

socialInvestment.name: Charity Bank
socialInvestment.notes: Bridges gap between grant confirmation and cash drawdown.

socialInvestment.name: Ulster Community Investment Trust (UCIT)
socialInvestment.notes: NI-focused social investment — first port of call for NI social investment.

stackingStrategy.stackingRatioTarget: 65% public grants / 20% philanthropy and diaspora / 10% social investment / 5% community shares
stackingStrategy.criticalPrinciple: NLHF will not fund 100% of any project. Build the match package first. Submit NLHF Stage 1 with confirmed or near-confirmed match.
